root_text.an4.62:1.1: Atha kho anāthapiṇḍiko gahapati yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho anāthapiṇḍikaṁ gahapatiṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.an4.62:2.1: “Cattārimāni, gahapati, sukhāni adhigamanīyāni gihinā kāmabhoginā kālena kālaṁ samayena samayaṁ upādāya.
root_text.an4.62:3.2: Idha, gahapati, kulaputtassa bhogā honti uṭṭhānavīriyādhigatā bāhābalaparicitā sedāvakkhittā dhammikā dhammaladdhā.
root_text.an4.62:3.3: So ‘bhogā me atthi uṭṭhānavīriyādhigatā bāhābalaparicitā sedāvakkhittā dhammikā dhammaladdhā’ti adhigacchati sukhaṁ, adhigacchati somanassaṁ.
root_text.an4.62:4.2: Idha, gahapati, kulaputto uṭṭhānavīriyādhigatehi bhogehi bāhābalaparicitehi sedāvakkhittehi dhammikehi dhammaladdhehi paribhuñjati puññāni ca karoti.
root_text.an4.62:4.3: So ‘uṭṭhānavīriyādhigatehi bhogehi bāhābalaparicitehi sedāvakkhittehi dhammikehi dhammaladdhehi paribhuñjāmi puññāni ca karomī’ti adhigacchati sukhaṁ, adhigacchati somanassaṁ.
root_text.an4.62:5.2: Idha, gahapati, kulaputto na kassaci kiñci dhāreti appaṁ vā bahuṁ vā.
root_text.an4.62:5.3: So ‘na kassaci kiñci dhāremi appaṁ vā bahuṁ vā’ti adhigacchati sukhaṁ, adhigacchati somanassaṁ.
root_text.an4.62:6.2: Idha, gahapati, ariyasāvako anavajjena kāyakammena samannāgato hoti, anavajjena vacīkammena samannāgato hoti, anavajjena manokammena samannāgato hoti.
root_text.an4.62:6.3: So ‘anavajjenamhi kāyakammena samannāgato, anavajjena vacīkammena samannāgato, anavajjena manokammena samannāgato’ti adhigacchati sukhaṁ, adhigacchati somanassaṁ.
root_text.an4.62:6.5: Imāni kho, gahapati, cattāri sukhāni adhigamanīyāni gihinā kāmabhoginā kālena kālaṁ samayena samayaṁ upādāyāti.

translation_text.an4.62:1.1: Then the householder Anāthapiṇḍika went up to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to him:
translation_text.an4.62:2.1: “Householder, these four kinds of happiness can be earned by a layperson who enjoys sensual pleasures, depending on time and occasion.
translation_text.an4.62:2.3: The happiness of ownership, using wealth, debtlessness, and blamelessness.
translation_text.an4.62:3.1: And what is the happiness of ownership?
translation_text.an4.62:3.2: It’s when a gentleman owns legitimate wealth that he has earned by his own efforts and initiative, built up with his own hands, gathered by the sweat of the brow.
translation_text.an4.62:3.3: When he reflects on this, he’s filled with pleasure and happiness.
translation_text.an4.62:3.4: This is called ‘the happiness of ownership’.
translation_text.an4.62:4.1: And what is the happiness of using wealth?
translation_text.an4.62:4.2: It’s when a gentleman uses his legitimate wealth, and makes merit with it.
translation_text.an4.62:4.3: When he reflects on this, he’s filled with pleasure and happiness.
translation_text.an4.62:4.4: This is called ‘the happiness of using wealth’.
translation_text.an4.62:5.1: And what is the happiness of debtlessness?
translation_text.an4.62:5.2: It’s when a gentleman owes no debt, large or small, to anyone.
translation_text.an4.62:5.3: When he reflects on this, he’s filled with pleasure and happiness.
translation_text.an4.62:5.4: This is called ‘the happiness of debtlessness’.
translation_text.an4.62:6.1: And what is the happiness of blamelessness?
translation_text.an4.62:6.2: It’s when a noble disciple has blameless conduct by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an4.62:6.3: When he reflects on this, he’s filled with pleasure and happiness.
translation_text.an4.62:6.4: This is called ‘the happiness of blamelessness’.
translation_text.an4.62:6.5: These four kinds of happiness can be earned by a layperson who enjoys sensual pleasures, depending on time and occasion.
translation_text.an4.62:7.2: and the extra happiness of possession,
translation_text.an4.62:7.3: a mortal enjoying the happiness of using wealth,
translation_text.an4.62:8.1: Discerning clearly, a clever person knows
translation_text.an4.62:8.3: the other kind is not worth a sixteenth part

variant_text.an4.62:5.2: kassaci kiñci dhāreti → kiñci vā deti (mr)
variant_text.an4.62:7.1: Ānaṇyasukhaṁ ñatvāna → anaṇaṁ sukhaṁ ñatvāna (bj, sya-all); anavajjasukhaṁ ñatvā (si)
